A three-chapter contemporary novella following June, a piano tuner in
her late fifties, across three house calls in one Tuesday in upstate
New York. Each house contains someone who was a significant person to
her once and no longer is. The piano is the excuse; the conversation
is the plot.

Voice is close-third, spare, interiority-forward. The book is about
what remains when two people no longer share a present tense.
